note: Registry of approved external skills. Empty by default; add entries through the onboarding process, not by popularity alone.

last_audit.approval_state: PROPOSED - every entry is reviewer-only and awaits operator approval. Nothing here has been promoted to workspace-approved.
last_audit.auditor_caveat: scripts/audit_external_skill.py scores frontmatter + boilerplate section presence, not depth. Every vendored skill below was retro-fitted with generic factory-contract sections (## Inputs / ## Procedure / ## Quality bar ...) by scripts/fix_skill_contracts.py, so a score >= 70 proves shape, NOT that the Skill Depth Rubric is satisfied. The depth_rubric block on each entry is a manual read of SKILL.md and is the load-bearing verdict.

values.generic: present but not specific to this repo (stack-agnostic prose)
values.boilerplate: auto-injected placeholder text with no real content
values.wrong-stack: present but written for a stack this repo does not use

depth_rubric_legend.rule: Plan Part C: reject if any criterion is blank. Nothing below is fully clean, hence reviewer-only for all.

skills.name: schemathesis
skills.description: Property-based API contract fuzzing driven from an OpenAPI schema; used against the app's /openapi.json in the blocking `make api-contract` CI job.
skills.version_or_commit: 4.10.2 (resolved by uv into the `quality` extra; verified from .venv dist-info METADATA)
skills.install_method: uv sync --extra quality  (dev-only; NOT in runtime dependencies, so it never ships in the Docker image)
supported_agents: n/a - library invoked by CI, not an agent skill
forbidden_operations: network (must run against the in-process ASGI app, never a live host)

depth_rubric.note: Rubric is scored against the REPO's usage (Makefile target `api-contract` + tests/contract), not against upstream docs. Exit-criteria / on-failure / thresholds (which checks, stateful?, --max-examples) are ledger item RB-8 and are owned by peer agent P0-F, not by this audit.

blocking_conditions: P0-F must pin the check set and --max-examples (RB-8); a schemathesis job with no configured checks is an artifact-present-but-empty gate (EN-2).
Resolves to v4.x - the v4 pytest/CLI API differs from v3; do not copy v3 recipes.
skills.reason: The one true drop-in from the external-tooling survey (plan Part B #7 / Part C). Closes the API-contract-fuzz hole.

skills.name: addyosmani/doubt-driven-development
skills.description: Adversarial 'assume it is wrong' review pass over a claim of completion; complements the repo's evidence-first / verification-before-completion doctrine.
skills.path: doubt-driven-development/ (upstream folder name; the R2 plan calls it `doubt-driven` - the plan's name is wrong)
skills.license: MIT (repository LICENSE, read from the GitHub repo page on 2026-07-19)
skills.install_method: not installed; would need vendoring into .agents/skills/ + a symlink from .claude/skills/
skills.validation_command: python3 scripts/audit_external_skill.py .agents/skills/doubt-driven-development  # CANNOT RUN - folder does not exist

depth_rubric.note: I could NOT audit this skill: it is not present anywhere on this machine (searched .agents/skills, .claude/skills, ~/.claude/skills, ~/.claude/plugins). Only the repo's existence and MIT license were verified, by fetching the GitHub repo page.

skills.verdict: WRAP, reviewer-only, PENDING VENDOR+AUDIT. Do not treat as available.
blocking_conditions: Must be vendored at a pinned commit and pass scripts/audit_external_skill.py before any use.
It is prose-only guidance: it is INFLUENCE, not a gate (plan Part B0). It must not be counted as enforcement machinery.
skills.reason: Plan Part C lists it under Wrap (reviewer-only). Its doubt discipline overlaps the repo's own `fanatic-critic` + `verification-before-completion` skills - adopt only if it adds something those do not.

skills.name: addyosmani/spec-driven-development
skills.description: Spec-first workflow: write the executable spec before the implementation; relevant to S2-S4 where thresholds must be specified before they are calibrated.
skills.path: spec-driven-development/ (upstream folder name; the R2 plan calls it `spec-driven`)
skills.license: MIT (repository LICENSE, read from the GitHub repo page on 2026-07-19)
skills.install_method: not installed; would need vendoring into .agents/skills/ + a symlink from .claude/skills/
skills.validation_command: python3 scripts/audit_external_skill.py .agents/skills/spec-driven-development  # CANNOT RUN - folder does not exist

depth_rubric.note: Same as doubt-driven-development: not on disk, so unauditable. Provenance + license verified only.

skills.verdict: WRAP, reviewer-only, PENDING VENDOR+AUDIT. Likely REDUNDANT here.
blocking_conditions: Must be vendored at a pinned commit and pass scripts/audit_external_skill.py before use.

Overlap risk: this repo already mandates a spec-first lifecycle (AGENTS.md 'Mandatory lifecycle' + docs/10/12/17/18 + `living-spec-builder`, `implementation-planning`). Adopting a second, weaker spec doctrine risks a conflicting source of truth - AGENTS.md precedence #3 (approved source of truth) wins.
skills.reason: Plan Part C lists it under Wrap (reviewer-only). Recommend adopting only the parts that are NOT already covered by the factory lifecycle.

skills.name: superpowers/subagent-driven-development
skills.description: Executes an implementation plan by dispatching a fresh implementer subagent per task, with a per-task spec+quality review and a whole-branch review at the end.
skills.version_or_commit: UNKNOWN - the vendored copy carries no upstream commit, version or license in its frontmatter
skills.license: upstream repo is MIT (obra/superpowers LICENSE, read from the GitHub repo page 2026-07-19). The VENDORED COPY contains no license file or license frontmatter - provenance is inferred from body text, not proven.

depth_rubric.note: Real content (Pre-Flight Plan Review, Model Selection, Handling Reviewer items, File Handoffs, Red Flags), unlike the boilerplate-only skills. Gaps: it names no repo paths, no `uv run pytest` command, no fixture, and defines no gate - it is a coordination pattern, not enforcement.

skills.verdict: ADOPT reviewer-only. Already de facto in use (this Phase-0 parallel fan is exactly this pattern).
blocking_conditions: The audit flags a destructive_git pattern. Reviewer-only must be enforced by the harness/operator: this skill must never be granted git write powers. In this Phase-0 run the mitigation was explicit prompt-level bans on tree-mutating git commands - that is influence, not a gate.
Vendored copy is unpinned and unlicensed on disk. Re-vendor at a pinned commit with the upstream LICENSE, or the policy's `unclear_license_for_vendoring` hard-reject rule applies.
skills.reason: Plan Part C 'Wrap (reviewer-only)'. Highest practical value of the four skill candidates.

skills.name: wednesday-solutions/deploy-checklist
skills.description: Pre-deploy / post-deploy checklist: env vars, migrations, CI, rollback plan, smoke tests, monitoring.
skills.repo: wednesday-solutions/ai-agent-skills (inferred from `metadata.author: wednesday-solutions`; NOT verified against the upstream repo)
skills.license: MIT (declared in the vendored SKILL.md frontmatter `license: MIT`; no LICENSE file accompanies the copy)

depth_rubric.note: Only skill in the roster with a hard stack mismatch.

refit_required.summary: Every executable command in this skill is npm/Node. This repo is Python + uv + pytest, deployed to Fly.io. As written the skill would issue commands that do not exist here.
concrete_changes: frontmatter permissions: `Bash(npm run format:check)` -> covered by `make quality`; drop the separate entry.
frontmatter permissions: `Bash(npm run test)` -> `Bash(uv run pytest -q)` (and `Bash(make validate)` for the gate chain).
frontmatter permissions: `Bash(npm run build)` -> `Bash(docker build .)` or drop (there is no JS build step; the deployable is the Docker image / Fly release).
'Tools' table row 'Run lint, test, build scripts' -> name the real targets: `make validate`, `make quality`, `make perf-gate`, `make api-contract`, `make fr-completeness`.

Health check `curl -s <url>/health` -> the repo's actual health path must be named explicitly (do not leave `<url>` a placeholder), and the check must be operator-run, not agent-run, because reviewer-only forbids network.
'Error rate in monitoring (Datadog, Grafana, Sentry)' -> replace with this repo's actual observability surface per docs/80-observability.md; the three named vendors are not used here.

Add the repo-specific pre-deploy gates the checklist is missing entirely: scripts/deploy_gate.py, scripts/security_scan.py, the e2e invariant gates (e2e/tests/invariants/), and the OPENROUTER_LIVE_EXECUTION_ENABLED / cost-band guardrails.
Add the repo's PII rule (never log or persist query text or provider prose) to 'Forbidden actions'.

Rollback thresholds ('error rate > 1%', 'P95 latency > 2x baseline') are UNMEASURED for this service. Per memory `guardrail-values-need-measurement` they must be re-derived from a measured baseline or explicitly marked as unmeasured placeholders - I did not measure them.

skills.verdict: RE-FIT then reviewer-only. REJECT as-is: its commands are for a stack this repo does not have, and a deploy checklist that green-lights on commands that cannot run is worse than no checklist.
blocking_conditions: Do not activate until the re-fit above lands; until then it is discovery-only in practice.
Deploy is a side-effecting operation - this skill must never hold deploy or network powers under reviewer-only.
skills.reason: Plan Part C 'Re-fit: deploy-checklist (npm->uv/pytest) before trusting'.

skills.name: superpowers/systematic-debugging
skills.description: Root-cause-first debugging discipline (the Iron Law: no fix before the root cause is proven).
skills.repo: obra/superpowers (inferred from body text and CREATION-LOG.md; not proven by any metadata in the vendored copy)
skills.license: upstream MIT (verified on the GitHub repo page 2026-07-19); vendored copy carries no license file

depth_rubric.note: Rich real content (Iron Law, Four Phases, Common Rationalizations) but its '## When to use' section is the auto-injected placeholder ('When the user asks about ') - the trigger criterion is literally blank.

skills.verdict: ADOPT reviewer-only. Already in the tree and consistent with repo doctrine; fix the blank trigger.
blocking_conditions: Replace the placeholder '## When to use' bullets with a real trigger before the rubric can be called satisfied.
skills.reason: Registering an already-vendored, already-used skill that was never in the registry - closing a governance gap found during this audit.

skills.name: e2e-testing-patterns
skills.description: Playwright/Cypress end-to-end testing patterns, flake handling and debugging.
skills.source_url: UNVERIFIED - no source recorded anywhere in the tree
skills.license: UNKNOWN - no license file, no license frontmatter, no provenance. Policy hard-reject rule `unclear_license_for_vendoring` applies.

depth_rubric.note: Stack-agnostic. It does not know about e2e/tests/invariants/, e2e/fixtures/golden-run.ts, or the blocking rendering-invariant gate - i.e. it is unaware of this repo's actual E2E contract (AGENTS.md 'UI verification').

skills.verdict: KEEP reviewer-only, but LOW VALUE as-is; provenance/license must be resolved or it should be removed.
blocking_conditions: Unknown license on a vendored file set - resolve or delete (policy hard-reject: unclear_license_for_vendoring).
Would need repo-specific paths/fixtures to be worth more than the repo's own AGENTS.md UI-verification section.
skills.reason: Already vendored and listed in plan Part C 'Have/use'; registering it makes the licensing gap visible instead of silent.

skills.name: anthropic/webapp-testing
skills.description: Drive and test a local web app with native Python Playwright scripts; screenshots and browser logs.
skills.repo: anthropics/skills (matched byte-for-byte against the local anthropic-agent-skills marketplace copy at ~/.claude/plugins/marketplaces/anthropic-agent-skills/skills/webapp-testing/SKILL.md - the only diff is the factory's injected boilerplate sections)
skills.version_or_commit: UNKNOWN - unpinned (content verified identical to the local marketplace copy)
skills.license: Apache-2.0 (LICENSE.txt shipped with the vendored copy)

depth_rubric.note: Best provenance in the roster. It launches a local server and a browser - side-effecting on localhost only; must stay off any deployed URL under reviewer-only.

blocking_conditions: Local-only: never point it at a deployed environment. It is an investigation aid, not a gate - the binding UI gate is e2e/tests/invariants/ in CI.
skills.reason: Already vendored, official Anthropic source, license present; registering closes the governance gap.

skills.name: wednesday-solutions/codebase-intel
skills.description: Codebase structure/risk/dependency Q&A and pre-edit blast-radius checks.
skills.repo: wednesday-solutions/ai-agent-skills (inferred from the `wednesday-skills` CLI and `.wednesday/` paths it requires; NOT verified upstream)
skills.license: UNKNOWN - no license file and no license frontmatter in the vendored copy.

depth_rubric.note: MEASURED BLOCKER: it depends on a `wednesday-skills` CLI and a `.wednesday/codebase/MASTER.md` index. Neither exists here - `which wednesday-skills` -> not found, `.wednesday/` -> No such file or directory. Its highest audit score (90) with the lowest usability is the clearest demonstration that the audit script scores shape, not depth.

skills.verdict: REJECT for now (non-executable in this repo) - keep registered as reviewer-only prose so the dead dependency is on the record.
blocking_conditions: Requires an uninstalled third-party CLI; installing it is a new supply-chain decision needing operator approval.
skills.reason: Registering an already-vendored skill whose tooling is absent, so the gap is visible rather than silently failing at use time.
